title: fritillaria crassifolia subsp. poluninii
intro: Fritillaria crassifolia subsp. poluninii is a subspecies of flowering plant in the lily family (Liliaceae), native to the high-altitude alpine meadows of the Himalayas. Traditionally, Fritillaria crassifolia subsp. poluninii has been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of various infections. Fritillaria crassifolia subsp. poluninii is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is also used in traditional Tibetan medicine to promote overall well-being and vitality.
